name: instant-api — idempotency_replay_refunded spike (likely upstream brownout driving agent retries)
description: P2 warning. instant_idempotency_replay_refunded_total ticks every time the Idempotency middleware serves a cached response and refunds the per-fingerprint daily rate-limit counter (FINDING API-1, fix 2026-05-29). A steady non-zero rate is healthy — agents retry transient 5xx with the same Idempotency-Key and we honour the Stripe-shape replay contract. A sudden spike on one route correlates with an upstream brownout (provisioner stalling, customer-DB pool exhaustion, NATS unauthorized). When this fires, flip to NR and check the corresponding 5xx rate for the same route — the agent population is hammering retries and the cached responses are the buffer keeping the funnel intact. Source: api/internal/metrics/metrics.go (IdempotencyReplayRefunded), emit site api/internal/middleware/rate_limit.go (RefundRateLimitCounter). Threshold is intentionally noisier than a page — fire WARNING on >50 refunds in 10 minutes so the operator can correlate against a 5xx spike.

nrql.query: SELECT sum(instant_idempotency_replay_refunded_total) FROM Metric WHERE metricName = 'instant_idempotency_replay_refunded_total' FACET route
